NKJV.1: When He had come down from the mountain, great multitudes followed Him.
NKJV.2: And behold, a leper came and worshiped Him, saying, “Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.”
NKJV.3: Then Jesus put out His hand and touched him, saying, “I am willing; be cleansed.” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
NKJV.4: And Jesus said to him, “See that you tell no one; but go your way, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that  Moses  commanded, as a testimony to them.”
NKJV.5: Now when Jesus had entered Capernaum, a centurion came to Him, pleading with Him,
NKJV.6: saying, “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, dreadfully tormented.”
NKJV.7: And Jesus said to him, “I will come and heal him.”
NKJV.8: The centurion answered and said, “Lord, I am not worthy that You should come under my roof. But only speak a word, and my servant will be healed.
NKJV.9: For I also am a man under authority, having soldiers under me. And I say to this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
NKJV.10: When Jesus heard it, He marveled, and said to those who followed, “Assuredly, I say to you, I have not found such great faith, not even in Israel!
NKJV.11: And I say to you that  many will come from east and west, and sit down with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
NKJV.12: But  the sons of the kingdom  will be cast out into outer darkness. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
NKJV.13: Then Jesus said to the centurion, “Go your way; and as you have believed, so let it be done for you.” And his servant was healed that same hour.
NKJV.14: Now when Jesus had come into Peter’s house, He saw his wife’s mother lying sick with a fever.
NKJV.15: So He touched her hand, and the fever left her. And she arose and served them.
NKJV.16: When evening had come, they brought to Him many who were demon-possessed. And He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were sick,
NKJV.17: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken by Isaiah the prophet, saying:  “He Himself took our infirmities And bore our sicknesses.”
NKJV.18: And when Jesus saw great multitudes about Him, He gave a command to depart to the other side.
NKJV.19: Then a certain scribe came and said to Him, “Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go.”
NKJV.20: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.”
NKJV.21: Then another of His disciples said to Him, “Lord, let me first go and bury my father.”
NKJV.22: But Jesus said to him, “Follow Me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
NKJV.23: Now when He got into a boat, His disciples followed Him.
NKJV.24: And suddenly a great tempest arose on the sea, so that the boat was covered with the waves. But He was asleep.
NKJV.25: Then His disciples came to Him and awoke Him, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing!”
NKJV.26: But He said to them, “Why are you fearful, O you of little faith?” Then He arose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm.
NKJV.27: So the men marveled, saying, “Who can this be, that even the winds and the sea obey Him?”
NKJV.28: When He had come to the other side, to the country of the Gergesenes, there met Him two demon-possessed men, coming out of the tombs, exceedingly fierce, so that no one could pass that way.
NKJV.29: And suddenly they cried out, saying, “What have we to do with You, Jesus, You Son of God? Have You come here to torment us before the time?”
NKJV.30: Now a good way off from them there was a herd of many swine feeding.
NKJV.31: So the demons begged Him, saying, “If You cast us out, permit us to go away into the herd of swine.”
NKJV.32: And He said to them, “Go.” So when they had come out, they went into the herd of swine. And suddenly the whole herd of swine ran violently down the steep place into the sea, and perished in the water.
NKJV.33: Then those who kept them fled; and they went away into the city and told everything, including what had happened to the demon-possessed men.
NKJV.34: And behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus. And when they saw Him, they begged Him to depart from their region.

ESV.1: When he came down from the mountain, great crowds followed him.
ESV.2: And behold, a leper  came to him and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, if you will, you can make me clean.”
ESV.3: And Jesus  stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, “I will; be clean.” And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
ESV.4: And Jesus said to him,  “See that you say nothing to anyone, but go, show yourself to the priest and offer the gift that Moses commanded, for a proof to them.”
ESV.5: When he had entered Capernaum, a centurion came forward to him, appealing to him,
ESV.6: “Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, suffering terribly.”
ESV.7: And he said to him, “I will come and heal him.”
ESV.8: But the centurion replied, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come under my roof, but only say the word, and my servant will be healed.
ESV.9: For I too am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. And I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my servant,  ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
ESV.10: When Jesus heard this, he marveled and said to those who followed him, “Truly, I tell you, with no one in Israel  have I found such faith.
ESV.11: I tell you, many will come from east and west and recline at table with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven,
ESV.12: while the sons of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
ESV.13: And to the centurion Jesus said, “Go; let it be done for you as you have believed.” And the servant was healed at that very moment.
ESV.14: And when Jesus entered Peter's house, he saw his mother-in-law lying sick with a fever.
ESV.15: He touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she rose and began to serve him.
ESV.16: That evening they brought to him many who were oppressed by demons, and he cast out the spirits with a word and healed all who were sick.
ESV.17: This was to fulfill what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah: “He took our illnesses and bore our diseases.”
ESV.18: Now when Jesus saw a crowd around him, he gave orders to go over to the other side.
ESV.19: And a scribe came up and said to him, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
ESV.20: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
ESV.21: Another of the disciples said to him, “Lord, let me first go and bury my father.”
ESV.22: And Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.”
ESV.23: And when he got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
ESV.24: And behold, there arose a great storm on the sea, so that the boat was being swamped by the waves; but he was asleep.
ESV.25: And they went and woke him, saying, “Save us, Lord; we are perishing.”
ESV.26: And he said to them, “Why are you afraid, O you of little faith?” Then he rose and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was a great calm.
ESV.27: And the men marveled, saying, “What sort of man is this, that even winds and sea obey him?”
ESV.28: And when he came to the other side, to the country of the Gadarenes,  two demon-possessed  men met him, coming out of the tombs, so fierce that no one could pass that way.
ESV.29: And behold, they cried out, “What have you to do with us, O Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
ESV.30: Now a herd of many pigs was feeding at some distance from them.
ESV.31: And the demons begged him, saying, “If you cast us out, send us away into the herd of pigs.”
ESV.32: And he said to them, “Go.” So they came out and went into the pigs, and behold, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned in the waters.
ESV.33: The herdsmen fled, and going into the city they told everything, especially what had happened to the demon-possessed men.
ESV.34: And behold, all the city came out to meet Jesus, and when they saw him, they begged him to leave their region.

NASB.1: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed Him.
NASB.2: And a leper came to Him and bowed down before Him, and said, 'Lord, if You are willing, You can make me clean.'
NASB.3: Jesus stretched out His hand and touched him, saying, 'I am willing; be cleansed.' And immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
NASB.4: And Jesus said to him, 'See that you tell no one; but go, show yourself to the priest and present the offering that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.'
NASB.5: And when Jesus entered Capernaum, a centurion came to Him, imploring Him,
NASB.6: and saying, 'Lord, my servant is lying paralyzed at home, fearfully tormented.'
NASB.7: Jesus said to him, 'I will come and heal him.'
NASB.8: But the centurion said, 'Lord, I am not worthy for You to come under my roof, but just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
NASB.9: 'For I also am a man under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to this one, 'Go!' and he goes, and to another, 'Come!' and he comes, and to my slave, 'Do this!' and he does [it].'
NASB.10: Now when Jesus heard [this], He marveled and said to those who were following, 'Truly I say to you, I have not found such great faith with anyone in Israel.
NASB.11: 'I say to you that many will come from east and west, and recline [at the table] with Abraham, Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven;
NASB.12: but the sons of the kingdom will be cast out into the outer darkness; in that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.'
NASB.13: And Jesus said to the centurion, 'Go; it shall be done for you as you have believed.' And the servant was healed that [very] moment.
NASB.14: When Jesus came into Peter's home, He saw his mother-in-law lying sick in bed with a fever.
NASB.15: He touched her hand, and the fever left her; and she got up and waited on Him.
NASB.16: When evening came, they brought to Him many who were demon-possessed; and He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were ill.
NASB.17: [This was] to fulfill what was spoken through Isaiah the prophet: 'HE HIMSELF TOOK OUR INFIRMITIES AND CARRIED AWAY OUR DISEASES.'
NASB.18: Now when Jesus saw a crowd around Him, He gave orders to depart to the other side [of the sea].
NASB.19: Then a scribe came and said to Him, 'Teacher, I will follow You wherever You go.'
NASB.20: Jesus said to him, 'The foxes have holes and the birds of the air [have] nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head.'
NASB.21: Another of the disciples said to Him, 'Lord, permit me first to go and bury my father.'
NASB.22: But Jesus said to him, 'Follow Me, and allow the dead to bury their own dead.'
NASB.23: When He got into the boat, His disciples followed Him.
NASB.24: And behold, there arose a great storm on the sea, so that the boat was being covered with the waves; but Jesus Himself was asleep.
NASB.25: And they came to [Him] and woke Him, saying, 'Save [us], Lord; we are perishing!'
NASB.26: He said to them, 'Why are you afraid, you men of little faith?' Then He got up and rebuked the winds and the sea, and it became perfectly calm.
NASB.27: The men were amazed, and said, 'What kind of a man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey Him?'
NASB.28: When He came to the other side into the country of the Gadarenes, two men who were demon-possessed met Him as they were coming out of the tombs. [They were] so extremely violent that no one could pass by that way.
NASB.29: And they cried out, saying, 'What business do we have with each other, Son of God? Have You come here to torment us before the time?'
NASB.30: Now there was a herd of many swine feeding at a distance from them.
NASB.31: The demons [began] to entreat Him, saying, 'If You [are] [going to] cast us out, send us into the herd of swine.'
NASB.32: And He said to them, 'Go!' And they came out and went into the swine, and the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and perished in the waters.
NASB.33: The herdsmen ran away, and went to the city and reported everything, including what had happened to the demoniacs.
NASB.34: And behold, the whole city came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw Him, they implored Him to leave their region.

NLT.1: Large crowds followed Jesus as he came down the mountainside.
NLT.2: Suddenly, a man with leprosy approached him and knelt before him. “Lord,” the man said, “if you are willing, you can heal me and make me clean.”
NLT.3: Jesus reached out and touched him. “I am willing,” he said. “Be healed!” And instantly the leprosy disappeared.
NLT.4: Then Jesus said to him, “Don’t tell anyone about this. Instead, go to the priest and let him examine you. Take along the offering required in the law of Moses for those who have been healed of leprosy. This will be a public testimony that you have been cleansed.”
NLT.5: When Jesus returned to Capernaum, a Roman officer came and pleaded with him,
NLT.6: “Lord, my young servant lies in bed, paralyzed and in terrible pain.”
NLT.7: Jesus said, “I will come and heal him.”
NLT.8: But the officer said, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come into my home. Just say the word from where you are, and my servant will be healed.
NLT.9: I know this because I am under the authority of my superior officers, and I have authority over my soldiers. I only need to say, ‘Go,’ and they go, or ‘Come,’ and they come. And if I say to my slaves, ‘Do this,’ they do it.”
NLT.10: When Jesus heard this, he was amazed. Turning to those who were following him, he said, “I tell you the truth, I haven’t seen faith like this in all Israel!
NLT.11: And I tell you this, that many Gentiles will come from all over the world — from east and west — and sit down with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob at the feast in the Kingdom of Heaven.
NLT.12: But many Israelites — those for whom the Kingdom was prepared — will be thrown into outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
NLT.13: Then Jesus said to the Roman officer, “Go back home. Because you believed, it has happened.” And the young servant was healed that same hour.
NLT.14: When Jesus arrived at Peter’s house, Peter’s mother-in-law was sick in bed with a high fever.
NLT.15: But when Jesus touched her hand, the fever left her. Then she got up and prepared a meal for him.
NLT.16: That evening many demon-possessed people were brought to Jesus. He cast out the evil spirits with a simple command, and he healed all the sick.
NLT.17:
This fulfilled the word of the Lord through the prophet Isaiah, who said, “He took our sicknesses
and removed our diseases.”
NLT.18: When Jesus saw the crowd around him, he instructed his disciples to cross to the other side of the lake.
NLT.19: Then one of the teachers of religious law said to him, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
NLT.20: But Jesus replied, “Foxes have dens to live in, and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place even to lay his head.”
NLT.21: Another of his disciples said, “Lord, first let me return home and bury my father.”
NLT.22: But Jesus told him, “Follow me now. Let the spiritually dead bury their own dead.”
NLT.23: Then Jesus got into the boat and started across the lake with his disciples.
NLT.24: Suddenly, a fierce storm struck the lake, with waves breaking into the boat. But Jesus was sleeping.
NLT.25: The disciples went and woke him up, shouting, “Lord, save us! We’re going to drown!”
NLT.26: Jesus responded, “Why are you afraid? You have so little faith!” Then he got up and rebuked the wind and waves, and suddenly there was a great calm.
NLT.27: The disciples were amazed. “Who is this man?” they asked. “Even the winds and waves obey him!”
NLT.28: When Jesus arrived on the other side of the lake, in the region of the Gadarenes, two men who were possessed by demons met him. They came out of the tombs and were so violent that no one could go through that area.
NLT.29: They began screaming at him, “Why are you interfering with us, Son of God? Have you come here to torture us before God’s appointed time?”
NLT.30: There happened to be a large herd of pigs feeding in the distance.
NLT.31: So the demons begged, “If you cast us out, send us into that herd of pigs.”
NLT.32: “All right, go!” Jesus commanded them. So the demons came out of the men and entered the pigs, and the whole herd plunged down the steep hillside into the lake and drowned in the water.
NLT.33: The herdsmen fled to the nearby town, telling everyone what happened to the demon-possessed men.
NLT.34: Then the entire town came out to meet Jesus, but they begged him to go away and leave them alone.

WEB.1: When he came down from the mountain, great multitudes followed him.
WEB.2: Behold, a leper came to him and worshiped him, saying, “Lord, if you want to, you can make me clean.”
WEB.3: Jesus stretched out his hand, and touched him, saying, “I want to. Be made clean.” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
WEB.4: Jesus said to him, “See that you tell nobody, but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
WEB.5: When he came into Capernaum, a centurion came to him, asking him,
WEB.6: and saying, “Lord, my servant lies in the house paralyzed, grievously tormented.”
WEB.7: Jesus said to him, “I will come and heal him.”
WEB.8: The centurion answered, “Lord, I’m not worthy for you to come under my roof. Just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
WEB.9: For I am also a man under authority, having under myself soldiers. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and tell another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and tell my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
WEB.10: When Jesus heard it, he marveled, and said to those who followed, “Most certainly I tell you, I haven’t found so great a faith, not even in Israel.
WEB.11: I tell you that many will come from the east and the west, and will sit down with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the Kingdom of Heaven,
WEB.12: but the children of the Kingdom will be thrown out into the outer darkness. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
WEB.13: Jesus said to the centurion, “Go your way. Let it be done for you as you have believed.” His servant was healed in that hour.
WEB.14: When Jesus came into Peter’s house, he saw his wife’s mother lying sick with a fever.
WEB.15: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. She got up and served him.
WEB.16: When evening came, they brought to him many possessed with demons. He cast out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were sick;
WEB.17: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying, “He took our infirmities, and bore our diseases.”
WEB.18: Now when Jesus saw great multitudes around him, he gave the order to depart to the other side.
WEB.19: A scribe came, and said to him, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
WEB.20: Jesus said to him, “The foxes have holes, and the birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
WEB.21: Another of his disciples said to him, “Lord, allow me first to go and bury my father.”
WEB.22: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and leave the dead to bury their own dead.”
WEB.23: When he got into a boat, his disciples followed him.
WEB.24: Behold, a violent storm came up on the sea, so much that the boat was covered with the waves, but he was asleep.
WEB.25: They came to him, and woke him up, saying, “Save us, Lord! We are dying!”
WEB.26: He said to them, “Why are you fearful, O you of little faith?”  Then he got up, rebuked the wind and the sea, and there was a great calm.
WEB.27: The men marveled, saying, “What kind of man is this, that even the wind and the sea obey him?”
WEB.28: When he came to the other side, into the country of the Gergesenes,  two people possessed by demons met him there, coming out of the tombs, exceedingly fierce, so that nobody could pass that way.
WEB.29: Behold, they cried out, saying, “What do we have to do with you, Jesus, Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
WEB.30: Now there was a herd of many pigs feeding far away from them.
WEB.31: The demons begged him, saying, “If you cast us out, permit us to go away into the herd of pigs.”
WEB.32: He said to them, “Go!” They came out, and went into the herd of pigs: and behold, the whole herd of pigs rushed down the cliff into the sea, and died in the water.
WEB.33: Those who fed them fled, and went away into the city, and told everything, including what happened to those who were possessed with demons.
WEB.34: Behold, all the city came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged that he would depart from their borders.

CSB17.1: When he came down from the mountain, large crowds <sup>ⓘ</sup> followed him.
CSB17.2: Right away <sup>ⓙ</sup> a man with leprosy <sup>[1]</sup> came up and knelt before him, <sup>ⓚ</sup> saying, “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.”
CSB17.3: Reaching out his hand, Jesus touched him, saying, “I am willing; be made clean.” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
CSB17.4: Then Jesus told him, “See that you don’t tell anyone; <sup>ⓛ</sup> but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.5: When he entered Capernaum, <sup>ⓝ</sup> a centurion came to him, pleading with him,
CSB17.6: “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, in terrible agony.”
CSB17.7: He said to him, “Am I to come and heal him?” <sup>[2]</sup>
CSB17.8: “Lord,” the centurion replied, “I am not worthy to have you come under my roof. But just say the word, and my servant will be healed. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.9: For I too am a man under authority, having soldiers under my command. <sup>[3]</sup> I say to this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes; and to my servant, ‘Do this!’ and he does it.”
CSB17.10: Hearing this, Jesus was amazed and said to those following him, “Truly I tell you, I have not found anyone in Israel with so great a faith.
CSB17.11: I tell you that many will come from east and west to share the banquet <sup>[4]</sup> with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.12: But the sons of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.13: Then Jesus told the centurion, “Go. As you have believed, <sup>ⓓ</sup>let it be done for you.” And his servant was healed that very moment. <sup>[5]</sup>
CSB17.14: Jesus went into Peter’s house <sup>ⓔ</sup> and saw his mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
CSB17.15: So he touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and began to serve him.
CSB17.16: When evening came, they brought to him many who were demon-possessed. He drove out the spirits <sup>ⓕ</sup> with a word and healed all who were sick, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.17:
so that what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah might be fulfilled: He himself took our weaknesses
and carried our diseases. [6] ⓗ
CSB17.18: When Jesus saw a large crowd <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓘ</sup> around him, he gave the order to go to the other side of the sea.
CSB17.19: A scribe approached him and said, <sup>ⓙ</sup> “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
CSB17.20: Jesus told him, “Foxes have dens, and birds of the sky have nests, but the Son of Man <sup>ⓚ</sup> has no place to lay his head.”
CSB17.21: “Lord,” another of his disciples said, “first let me go bury my father.”
CSB17.22: But Jesus told him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
CSB17.23: As he got into the boat, <sup>ⓛ</sup> his disciples <sup>ⓜ</sup> followed him.
CSB17.24: Suddenly, a violent storm arose on the sea, so that the boat was being swamped by the waves — but Jesus kept sleeping.
CSB17.25: So the disciples came and woke him up, saying, “Lord, save us! We’re going to die!”
CSB17.26: He said to them, “Why are you afraid, you of little faith?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the sea, <sup>ⓝ</sup> and there was a great calm.
CSB17.27: The men were amazed and asked, “What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the sea obey <sup>ⓐ</sup> him!”
CSB17.28: When he had come to the other side, <sup>ⓑ</sup> to the region of the Gadarenes, <sup>[8]</sup> two demon-possessed men met him as they came out of the tombs. They were so violent that no one could pass that way.
CSB17.29: Suddenly they shouted, “What do you have to do with us, <sup>[9]</sup> Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?” <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.30: A long way off from them, a large herd of pigs was feeding.
CSB17.31: “If you drive us out,” the demons begged him, “send us into the herd of pigs.”
CSB17.32: “Go!” he told them. So when they had come out, they entered the pigs, and the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and perished in the water.
CSB17.33: Then the men who tended them fled. They went into the city and reported everything, especially what had happened to those who were demon-possessed.
CSB17.34: At that, the whole town went out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their region. <sup>ⓓ</sup>

AMP.1: When Jesus came down from the mountain, large crowds followed Him.
AMP.2: And a leper came up to Him and bowed down before Him, saying, "Lord, if You are willing, You are able to make me clean (well)."
AMP.3: Jesus reached out His hand and touched him, saying, "I am willing; be cleansed." Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
AMP.4: And Jesus said to him, "See that you tell no one [about this]; but go, show yourself to the priest [for inspection] and present the offering that Moses commanded, as a testimony (evidence) to them [of your healing]."
AMP.5: As Jesus went into Capernaum, a centurion came up to Him, begging Him [for help],
AMP.6: and saying, "Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, with intense and terrible, tormenting pain."
AMP.7: Jesus said to him, "I will come and heal him."
AMP.8: But the centurion replied to Him, "Lord, I am not worthy to have You come under my roof, but only say the word, and my servant will be healed.
AMP.9: For I also am a man subject to authority [of a higher rank], with soldiers subject to me; and I say to one, 'Go!' and he goes, and to another, 'Come!' and he comes, and to my slave, 'Do this!' and he does it."
AMP.10: When Jesus heard this, He was amazed and said to those who were following Him, "I tell you truthfully, I have not found such great faith [as this] with anyone in Israel.
AMP.11: I say to you that many [Gentiles] will come from east and west, and will sit down [to feast at the table, and enjoy God's promises] with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven [because they accepted Me as Savior],
AMP.12: while the sons and heirs of the kingdom [the descendants of Abraham who will not recognize Me as Messiah] will be thrown out into the outer darkness; in that place [which is farthest removed from the kingdom] there will be weeping [in sorrow and pain] and grinding of teeth [in distress and anger]."
AMP.13: Then Jesus said to the centurion, "Go; it will be done for you as you have believed." And the servant was restored to health at that very hour.
AMP.14: When Jesus went into Peter's house [in Capernaum], He saw Peter's mother-in-law lying sick in bed with a fever.
AMP.15: He touched her hand and the fever left her; and she got up and served Him.
AMP.16: When evening came, they brought to Him many who were under the power of demons; and He cast out the evil spirits with a word, and restored to health all who were sick [exhibiting His authority as Messiah],
AMP.17: so that He fulfilled what was spoken by the prophet Isaiah: "He Himself took our infirmities [upon Himself] and carried away our diseases."
AMP.18: Now when Jesus saw a crowd around Him, He gave orders to cast off for the other side of the Sea of Galilee.
AMP.19: Then [on His way to board the boat] a scribe [who was a respected and authoritative interpreter of the Law] came and said to Him, "Master, I will accompany You [as Your student] wherever You go."
AMP.20: Jesus replied to him, "Foxes have holes and the birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay His head."
AMP.21: Another of the disciples said to Him, "Lord, let me first go and bury my father (collect my inheritance)."
AMP.22: But Jesus said to him, "Follow Me [believing in Me as Master and Teacher], and allow the [spiritually] dead to bury their own dead."
AMP.23: When He got into the boat, His disciples followed Him.
AMP.24: And suddenly a violent storm arose on the sea, so that the boat was being covered by the waves; but Jesus was sleeping.
AMP.25: And the disciples went and woke Him, saying, "Lord, save us, we are going to die!"
AMP.26: He said to them, "Why are you afraid, you men of little faith?" Then He got up and rebuked the winds and the sea, and there was [at once] a great and wonderful calm [a perfect peacefulness].
AMP.27: The men wondered in amazement, saying, "What kind of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey Him?"
AMP.28: When He arrived at the other side in the country of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men coming out of the tombs met Him. They were so extremely fierce and violent that no one could pass by that way.
AMP.29: And they screamed out, " What business do we have [in common] with each other, Son of God? Have You come to torment us before the appointed time [of judgment]?"
AMP.30: Some distance from them a large herd of pigs was grazing.
AMP.31: The demons began begging Him, "If You drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs."
AMP.32: And He said to them, "Go!" So they came out [of the men] and went into the pigs, and the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and died in the water.
AMP.33: The herdsmen ran away, and went to the city and reported everything, including what had happened to the men under the power of demons.
AMP.34: And the whole city came out to meet Jesus; and as soon as they saw Him, they begged Him to leave their region.

MSG.1: Jesus came down the mountain with the cheers of the crowd still ringing in his ears.
MSG.2: Then a leper appeared and went to his knees before Jesus, praying, "Master, if you want to, you can heal my body."
MSG.3: Jesus reached out and touched him, saying, "I want to. Be clean." Then and there, all signs of the leprosy were gone.
MSG.4: Jesus said, "Don't talk about this all over town. Just quietly present your healed body to the priest, along with the appropriate expressions of thanks to God. Your cleansed and grateful life, not your words, will bear witness to what I have done."
MSG.5: As Jesus entered the village of Capernaum, a Roman captain came up in a panic and said,
MSG.6: "Master, my servant is sick. He can't walk. He's in terrible pain."
MSG.7: Jesus said, "I'll come and heal him."
MSG.8: "Oh, no," said the captain. "I don't want to put you to all that trouble. Just give the order and my servant will be fine.
MSG.9: I'm a man who takes orders and gives orders. I tell one soldier, 'Go,' and he goes; to another, 'Come,' and he comes; to my slave, 'Do this,' and he does it."
MSG.10: Taken aback, Jesus said, "I've yet to come across this kind of simple trust in Israel, the very people who are supposed to know all about God and how he works.
MSG.11: This man is the vanguard of many outsiders who will soon be coming from all directions—streaming in from the east, pouring in from the west, sitting down at God's kingdom banquet alongside Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
MSG.12: Then those who grew up 'in the faith' but had no faith will find themselves out in the cold, outsiders to grace and wondering what happened."
MSG.13: Then Jesus turned to the captain and said, "Go. What you believed could happen has happened." At that moment his servant became well.
MSG.14: By this time they were in front of Peter's house. On entering, Jesus found Peter's mother-in-law sick in bed, burning up with fever.
MSG.15: He touched her hand and the fever was gone. No sooner was she up on her feet than she was fixing dinner for him.
MSG.16: That evening a lot of demon-afflicted people were brought to him. He relieved the inwardly tormented. He cured the bodily ill.
MSG.17: He fulfilled Isaiah's well-known sermon: He took our illnesses, He carried our diseases.
MSG.18: When Jesus saw that a curious crowd was growing by the minute, he told his disciples to get him out of there to the other side of the lake.
MSG.19: As they left, a religion scholar asked if he could go along. "I'll go with you, wherever," he said.
MSG.20: Jesus was curt: "Are you ready to rough it? We're not staying in the best inns, you know."
MSG.21: Another follower said, "Master, excuse me for a couple of days, please. I have my father's funeral to take care of."
MSG.22: Jesus refused. "First things first. Your business is life, not death. Follow me. Pursue life."
MSG.23: Then he got in the boat, his disciples with him.
MSG.24: The next thing they knew, they were in a severe storm. Waves were crashing into the boat—and he was sound asleep!
MSG.25: They roused him, pleading, "Master, save us! We're going down!"
MSG.26: Jesus reprimanded them. "Why are you such cowards, such faint-hearts?" Then he stood up and told the wind to be silent, the sea to quiet down: "Silence!" The sea became smooth as glass.
MSG.27: The men rubbed their eyes, astonished. "What's going on here? Wind and sea come to heel at his command!"
MSG.28: They landed in the country of the Gadarenes and were met by two madmen, victims of demons, coming out of the cemetery. The men had terrorized the region for so long that no one considered it safe to walk down that stretch of road anymore.
MSG.29: Seeing Jesus, the madmen screamed out, "What business do you have giving us a hard time? You're the Son of God! You weren't supposed to show up here yet!"
MSG.30: Off in the distance a herd of pigs was browsing and rooting.
MSG.31: The evil spirits begged Jesus, "If you kick us out of these men, let us live in the pigs."
MSG.32: Jesus said, "Go ahead, but get out of here!" Crazed, the pigs stampeded over a cliff into the sea and drowned.
MSG.33: Scared to death, the swineherds bolted. They told everyone back in town what had happened to the madmen and the pigs.
MSG.34: Those who heard about it were angry about the drowned pigs. A mob formed and demanded that Jesus get out and not come back.

NIV.1:
The Man With Leprosy
When he came down from the mountainside, large crowds followed him.
NIV.2: A man with leprosy came and knelt before him and said, “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.”
NIV.3: Jesus reached out his hand and touched the man. “I am willing,” he said. “Be clean!” Immediately he was cured of his leprosy.
NIV.4: Then Jesus said to him, “See that you don’t tell anyone. But go, show yourself to the priest and offer the gift Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
NIV.5:
The Faith of the Centurion
When Jesus had entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, asking for help.
NIV.6: “Lord,” he said, “my servant lies at home paralyzed and in terrible suffering.”
NIV.7: Jesus said to him, “I will go and heal him.”
NIV.8: The centurion replied, “Lord, I do not deserve to have you come under my roof. But just say the word, and my servant will be healed.
NIV.9: For I myself am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I tell this one, ‘Go,’ and he goes; and that one, ‘Come,’ and he comes. I say to my servant, ‘Do this,’ and he does it.”
NIV.10: When Jesus heard this, he was astonished and said to those following him, “I tell you the truth, I have not found anyone in Israel with such great faith.
NIV.11: I say to you that many will come from the east and the west, and will take their places at the feast with Abraham, Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven.
NIV.12: But the subjects of the kingdom will be thrown outside, into the darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
NIV.13: Then Jesus said to the centurion, “Go! It will be done just as you believed it would.” And his servant was healed at that very hour.
NIV.14:
Jesus Heals Many
When Jesus came into Peter’s house, he saw Peter’s mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever.
NIV.15: He touched her hand and the fever left her, and she got up and began to wait on him.
NIV.16: When evening came, many who were demon-possessed were brought to him, and he drove out the spirits with a word and healed all the sick.
NIV.17:
This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
“He took up our infirmities
and carried our diseases.”
NIV.18:
The Cost of Following Jesus
When Jesus saw the crowd around him, he gave orders to cross to the other side of the lake.
NIV.19: Then a teacher of the law came to him and said, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
NIV.20: Jesus replied, “Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”
NIV.21: Another disciple said to him, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
NIV.22: But Jesus told him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
NIV.23:
Jesus Calms the Storm
Then he got into the boat and his disciples followed him.
NIV.24: Without warning, a furious storm came up on the lake, so that the waves swept over the boat. But Jesus was sleeping.
NIV.25: The disciples went and woke him, saying, “Lord, save us! We’re going to drown!”
NIV.26: He replied, “You of little faith, why are you so afraid?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the waves, and it was completely calm.
NIV.27: The men were amazed and asked, “What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the waves obey him!”
NIV.28:
The Healing of Two Demon-possessed Men
When he arrived at the other side in the region of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men coming from the tombs met him. They were so violent that no one could pass that way.
NIV.29: “What do you want with us, Son of God?” they shouted. “Have you come here to torture us before the appointed time?”
NIV.30: Some distance from them a large herd of pigs was feeding.
NIV.31: The demons begged Jesus, “If you drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs.”
NIV.32: He said to them, “Go!” So they came out and went into the pigs, and the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the lake and died in the water.
NIV.33: Those tending the pigs ran off, went into the town and reported all this, including what had happened to the demon-possessed men.
NIV.34: Then the whole town went out to meet Jesus. And when they saw him, they pleaded with him to leave their region.

NRSVCE.1: When Jesus had come down from the mountain, great crowds followed him;
NRSVCE.2: and there was a leper who came to him and knelt before him, saying, “Lord, if you choose, you can make me clean.”
NRSVCE.3: He stretched out his hand and touched him, saying, “I do choose. Be made clean!” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
NRSVCE.4: Then Jesus said to him, “See that you say nothing to anyone; but go, show yourself to the priest, and offer the gift that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
NRSVCE.5: When he entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, appealing to him
NRSVCE.6: and saying, “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, in terrible distress.”
NRSVCE.7: And he said to him, “I will come and cure him.”
NRSVCE.8: The centurion answered, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come under my roof; but only speak the word, and my servant will be healed.
NRSVCE.9: For I also am a man under authority, with soldiers under me; and I say to one, ‘Go,’ and he goes, and to another, ‘Come,’ and he comes, and to my slave, ‘Do this,’ and the slave does it.”
NRSVCE.10: When Jesus heard him, he was amazed and said to those who followed him, “Truly I tell you, in no one in Israel have I found such faith.
NRSVCE.11: I tell you, many will come from east and west and will eat with Abraham and Isaac and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven,
NRSVCE.12: while the heirs of the kingdom will be thrown into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
NRSVCE.13: And to the centurion Jesus said, “Go; let it be done for you according to your faith.” And the servant was healed in that hour.
NRSVCE.14: When Jesus entered Peter’s house, he saw his mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever;
NRSVCE.15: he touched her hand, and the fever left her, and she got up and began to serve him.
NRSVCE.16: That evening they brought to him many who were possessed with demons; and he cast out the spirits with a word, and cured all who were sick.
NRSVCE.17: This was to fulfill what had been spoken through the prophet Isaiah, “He took our infirmities and bore our diseases.”
NRSVCE.18: Now when Jesus saw great crowds around him, he gave orders to go over to the other side.
NRSVCE.19: A scribe then approached and said, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
NRSVCE.20: And Jesus said to him, “Foxes have holes, and birds of the air have nests; but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.”
NRSVCE.21: Another of his disciples said to him, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
NRSVCE.22: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
NRSVCE.23: And when he got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
NRSVCE.24: A windstorm arose on the sea, so great that the boat was being swamped by the waves; but he was asleep.
NRSVCE.25: And they went and woke him up, saying, “Lord, save us! We are perishing!”
NRSVCE.26: And he said to them, “Why are you afraid, you of little faith?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the sea; and there was a dead calm.
NRSVCE.27: They were amazed, saying, “What sort of man is this, that even the winds and the sea obey him?”
NRSVCE.28: When he came to the other side, to the country of the Gadarenes, two demoniacs coming out of the tombs met him. They were so fierce that no one could pass that way.
NRSVCE.29: Suddenly they shouted, “What have you to do with us, Son of God? Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
NRSVCE.30: Now a large herd of swine was feeding at some distance from them.
NRSVCE.31: The demons begged him, “If you cast us out, send us into the herd of swine.”
NRSVCE.32: And he said to them, “Go!” So they came out and entered the swine; and suddenly, the whole herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and perished in the water.
NRSVCE.33: The swineherds ran off, and on going into the town, they told the whole story about what had happened to the demoniacs.
NRSVCE.34: Then the whole town came out to meet Jesus; and when they saw him, they begged him to leave their neighborhood.

NET.1: After he came down from the mountain, large crowds followed him.
NET.2: And a leper approached, and bowed low before him, saying, “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.”
NET.3: He stretched out his hand and touched him saying, “I am willing. Be clean!” Immediately his leprosy was cleansed.
NET.4: Then Jesus said to him, “See that you do not speak to anyone, but go, show yourself to a priest, and bring the offering that Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.”
NET.5: When he entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him asking for help:
NET.6: “Lord, my servant is lying at home paralyzed, in terrible anguish.”
NET.7: Jesus said to him, “I will come and heal him.”
NET.8: But the centurion replied, “Lord, I am not worthy to have you come under my roof. Instead, just say the word and my servant will be healed.
NET.9: For I too am a man under authority, with soldiers under me. I say to this one, ‘Go’ and he goes, and to another ‘Come’ and he comes, and to my slave ‘Do this’ and he does it.”
NET.10: When Jesus heard this he was amazed and said to those who followed him, “I tell you the truth, I have not found such faith in anyone in Israel!
NET.11: I tell you, many will come from the east and west to share the banquet with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob in the kingdom of heaven,
NET.12: but the sons of the kingdom will be thrown out into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”
NET.13: Then Jesus said to the centurion, “Go; just as you believed, it will be done for you.” And the servant was healed at that hour.
NET.14: Now when Jesus entered Peter’s house, he saw his mother-in-law lying down, sick with a fever.
NET.15: He touched her hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and began to serve them.
NET.16: When it was evening, many demon-possessed people were brought to him. He drove out the spirits with a word, and healed all who were sick.
NET.17: In this way what was spoken by Isaiah the prophet was fulfilled: “He took our weaknesses and carried our diseases.”
NET.18: Now when Jesus saw a large crowd around him, he gave orders to go to the other side of the lake.
NET.19: Then an expert in the law came to him and said, “Teacher, I will follow you wherever you go.”
NET.20: Jesus said to him, “Foxes have dens, and the birds in the sky have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”
NET.21: Another of the disciples said to him, “Lord, let me first go and bury my father.”
NET.22: But Jesus said to him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
NET.23: As he got into the boat, his disciples followed him.
NET.24: And a great storm developed on the sea so that the waves began to swamp the boat. But he was asleep.
NET.25: So they came and woke him up saying, “Lord, save us! We are about to die!”
NET.26: But he said to them, “Why are you cowardly, you people of little faith?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the sea, and it was dead calm.
NET.27: And the men were amazed and said, “What sort of person is this? Even the winds and the sea obey him!”
NET.28: When he came to the other side, to the region of the Gadarenes, two demon-possessed men coming from the tombs met him. They were extremely violent, so that no one was able to pass by that way.
NET.29: They cried out, “Son of God, leave us alone! Have you come here to torment us before the time?”
NET.30: A large herd of pigs was feeding some distance from them.
NET.31: Then the demons begged him, “If you drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs.”
NET.32: And he said, “Go!” So they came out and went into the pigs, and the herd rushed down the steep slope into the lake and drowned in the water.
NET.33: The herdsmen ran off, went into the town, and told everything that had happened to the demon-possessed men.
NET.34: Then the entire town came out to meet Jesus. And when they saw him, they begged him to leave their region.
